articles.title: Tamil Nadu government formation: Options and precedents before Governor Arlekar

A former Tamil Nadu official notes that seeking letters of support from MLAs is acceptable, but the Governor should not conduct a headcount. The strength of a party's claim to form the government should be demonstrated on the floor of the House. The piece highlights the importance of parliamentary conventions in state governance.
articles.raw_summary: A former official of the Tamil Nadu government, who is well-versed with parliamentary practices and conventions, explains that there is nothing wrong with the Governor seeking letters of support, but he or she should not carry out a head count. articles.title: Preliminary Verification Closure Not Equivalent To Closure Report Under CrPC: J&K&L High Court Refuses To Quash FIR
articles.summary: The Jammu & Kashmir and Ladakh High Court has held that the High Court cannot interfere under its inherent jurisdiction to quash an FIR merely because a preliminary verification had earlier recommended closure, when subsequent material collected during inquiry discloses the commission of a cognizable offence. The Court clarified that a preliminary verification closure measure only requires supplying brief reasons to the informant and does not amount to a closure report under Section 154/170 Cr.P

articles.title: S.294 CrPC | Accused Can Exhibit Documents Already Part Of Chargesheet Without Formal Proof Of Signature : Supreme Court
articles.summary: The Supreme Court ruled that accused individuals can present documents already included in the chargesheet without formally proving the authenticity of signatures on those documents. The Court relied on Section 294(3) of the Code of Criminal Procedure (CrPC), stating that such documents can be admitted as evidence without signature verification if their genuineness is not disputed. This decision streamlines legal proceedings by reducing procedural burdens in criminal trials.
articles.raw_summary: The Supreme Court has observed that when an accused seeks to exhibit on the defence side certain documents which already form part of the charge sheet and the prosecution's record, they need not undergo formal proof and can be read in evidence without proving the signature of the person purported to have signed them. The Court relied on Section 294(3) of the Code of Criminal Procedure, as per which, proof of signature is not required to take the document on evidence if its genuineness is not dis

This enforcement action highlights tensions between local governance bodies and official data collection efforts.

articles.title: “Illegal But Not Immoral”: HP HC Grants Family Pension to Second Wife, Holds Economically Dependent Spouse Cannot Be Left Destitute
articles.summary: The Himachal Pradesh High Court ruled that a second wife, though not legally recognized as a spouse, is entitled to family pension if economically dependent on the deceased employee. The court held that rules must be interpreted to prevent destitution, emphasizing the law's purpose over rigid formalities. The judgment underscores the judiciary's role in ensuring social justice and protecting vulnerable dependents. The ruling directly impacts family welfare policies and inheritance laws in India.
articles.raw_summary: The Court s role is to understand the purpose of law in society and to assist the law in achieving its intended purpose. An interpretation which reduces the subordinate legislation to a futility should be avoided, and a view that the rule making authority made the rule only for the purpose of bringing about an effective result should be adopted. articles.summary: The Union Cabinet has approved a proposal to increase the number of Supreme Court judges from 34 to 38. Union Minister Ashwini Vaishnaw stated that a bill to formalize this change will be introduced in the next Parliament session. This decision aims to address the growing workload on the Supreme Court by expanding its judicial capacity.

This ruling highlights the importance of proper documentation in tax disputes and the tribunal's role in adjudicating such cases.

articles.title: Bihar Cabinet approves $500 million loan from World Bank for strengthening urban centres
articles.summary: The Bihar Cabinet approved a $500 million loan from the World Bank to strengthen urban centers. The funds will support AI-based road maintenance, boost subsidies under the PM-eBus Sewa scheme, and finance Ganga erosion control works. Additionally, a hospital in Sitamarhi was renamed. These decisions aim to improve urban infrastructure and public services in Bihar.
articles.raw_summary: Bihar Cabinet okays $500 million World Bank loan for urban projects, boosts PM-eBus Sewa subsidy, funds AI based road upkeep, renames Sitamarhi hospital, clears Ganga erosion works.

articles.title: Why Madras HC ordered granting full maternity leave to judicial employee for third pregnancy
articles.summary: The Madras High Court ruled that a judicial employee should receive full maternity leave for her third pregnancy, criticizing a 12-week limit as discriminatory. The court stated that suffering during pregnancy is the same regardless of the number of pregnancies and that a welfare state cannot deny equal benefits. The ruling highlights the need for equitable maternity leave policies in government employment.
articles.raw_summary: The court criticised a 12-week maternity leave limit on third pregnancies, saying 'suffering would be the same' and a 'welfare state' cannot discriminate in approving maternity benefits.

articles.summary: The court quashed GST refund orders after the Assessing Officer (AO) ignored a clarificatory amendment to Rule 89(5). The authorities have been directed to reconsider refund claims under the amended provisions.

articles.title: Cabinet clears proposal to make insult to ‘Vande Mataram’ a punishable offence: Report
articles.summary: The Indian Cabinet has approved a proposal to make insulting the national song 'Vande Mataram' a punishable offense, similar to existing laws for the national anthem, flag, and Constitution. Currently, insults to these symbols can lead to imprisonment of up to three years or a fine. The move aims to strengthen respect for national symbols. The proposal is part of a broader legal framework addressing disrespect to national icons.

The new structure simplifies the process, but the core tax rates and turnover limits remain largely unchanged. This change impacts small businesses, professionals, and taxpayers opting for presumptive taxation schemes.

articles.title: ITAT Remands Case as CIT(A) Admitted C-Forms Without Following Rule 46A(3)
articles.summary: The Income Tax Appellate Tribunal (ITAT) in Raipur ruled that the Commissioner of Income Tax (Appeals) [CIT(A)] erred by admitting C-Forms as additional evidence without giving the Assessing Officer an opportunity to examine them, as required under Rule 46A(3). articles.title: Filing GSTR-1 Without Paying GST Can Trigger Section 74 Fraud Proceedings
articles.summary: The Supreme Court ruled that repeatedly filing GSTR-1 (sales return) without paying GST, despite collecting tax amounts from customers, constitutes wilful suppression under Section 74 of the GST Act. The court rejected claims of financial hardship as a valid defense for non-compliance. This decision strengthens tax enforcement and may lead to fraud proceedings against defaulters. The ruling applies to businesses issuing invoices and collecting GST but failing to file GSTR-3B returns.
articles.raw_summary: The Supreme Court affirmed that continuous non-filing of GSTR-3B despite issuing invoices and collecting GST amounts to wilful suppression under Section 74. The ruling rejects financial hardship as a defence against GST compliance failures. The post Filing GSTR-1 Without Paying GST Can Trigger Section 74 Fraud Proceedings appeared first on TaxGuru .

articles.title: Exports of Iron Ore must be assessed on Wet Metric Tonne (WMT) basis and not on Dry Metric Tonne (DMT) basis
articles.summary: The article reports that for iron ore export transactions before the 2022 Finance Act amendment, the determination of iron ore fines must be based on Wet Metric Tonne (WMT) rather than Dry Metric Tonne (DMT). The Adjudicating Authority had used the DMT method, but this has been rejected. The ruling has implications for tax assessment and export policies in India.
articles.raw_summary: For export transactions occurring before the Finance Act, 2022 amendment, the determination of iron ore fines (Fe content) must be done on a Wet Metric Tonne (WMT) basis, rejecting the Dry Metric Tonne (DMT) method adopted by the Adjudicating Authority. articles.summary: The Income Tax Appellate Tribunal (ITAT) ruled that the full disallowance of purchases by the Assessing Officer (AO) was unjustified when sales and stock records were accepted. The ITAT reduced the disallowance to 12.5% of the disputed purchases due to the AO's lack of an independent enquiry. This decision impacts tax assessment practices and highlights the need for thorough verification in tax proceedings.
articles.raw_summary: The Tribunal held that full disallowance of purchases was not justified where corresponding sales and quantitative stock records were accepted. articles.title: Wrong Section Code Can’t Kill a Genuine Trust – ITAT Rescues 12A Registration from Hyper-Technical Rejection
articles.summary: The Income Tax Appellate Tribunal (ITAT) ruled that a genuine charitable trust should not lose its 12A registration due to a technical error in filing Form 10AB under the wrong section of the Income Tax Act. The decision highlights the judiciary's role in preventing hyper-technical rejections from undermining genuine charitable activities.

articles.title: “Not a single whisper of force”: Bombay HC Quashes POCSO Case After Accused Marries Minor Prosecutrix, Now 7 Months Pregnant
articles.summary: The Bombay High Court quashed a POCSO case after the accused married the minor prosecutrix, who is now seven months pregnant. The court held that no force or coercion was involved and that the victim had no issue staying with the accused and his family. The ruling highlights the legal complexities in cases involving minors under the POCSO Act. The decision underscores the need for careful application of child protection laws in such circumstances.
articles.raw_summary: There was not a single whisper that the accused forced the prosecutrix or threatened her or had sexual intercourse on false promise of marriage. Though the victim was minor, she did not have any problem in staying with the accused and his family. Therefore, the Court held that the criminal proceedings would cause injustice. articles.title: Fly Ash Sale Receipts Taxable as Accounting Treatment Cannot Override Tax Law: ITAT Chandigarh
articles.summary: The Income Tax Appellate Tribunal (ITAT) in Chandigarh ruled that receipts from the sale of fly ash are taxable as business income under the Income Tax Act. The tribunal clarified that accounting treatment cannot override tax law, even if the income is later credited to a statutory fund. The decision impacts power generation companies that sell fly ash as a byproduct of their operations.
articles.raw_summary: The ITAT Chandigarh ruled that receipts from sale of fly ash constituted taxable business income as they arose directly from the assessees power generation activity. The Tribunal held that subsequent credit to a statutory fund amounted only to application of income. The post Fly Ash Sale Receipts Taxable as Accounting Treatment Cannot Override Tax Law: ITAT Chandigarh appeared first on TaxGuru .

articles.title: ITAT Condones 2659-Day Delay Because Improper Tax Advice Prevented Timely Appeal
articles.summary: The Income Tax Appellate Tribunal (ITAT) in Pune allowed a 2659-day delay in an appeal because the taxpayer relied on incorrect tax advice from consultants, which prevented timely filing. The tribunal remanded the case for fresh adjudication on the Section 80P deduction claim. This ruling highlights the impact of legal advice on tax compliance and could influence future tax policy interpretations. The decision underscores the importance of proper legal counsel in tax matters.
articles.raw_summary: ITAT Pune held that delay caused by lack of proper legal advice and dependence on tax consultants constituted reasonable cause. articles.title: India should adopt dual-track trade strategy at WTO, say experts
articles.summary: Experts suggest India adopt a dual-track trade strategy. This involves defending World Trade Organisation rules while selectively joining plurilateral talks. Such initiatives shape global trade. India has concerns about plurilateral agreements weakening the multilateral system. However, recalibrating its approach could serve India's economic and strategic interests. Pragmatic, coalition-based approaches are needed to revitalize multilateralism.

articles.summary: The Assam Assembly elections were held on April 9, 2026, in a single phase. Badruddin Ajmal of the All India United Democratic Front (AIUDF) won the Binnakandi seat by 35,380 votes, defeating Rejaul Karim Chowdhury. The elections follow the 2023 delimitation of constituencies and occur as the National Democratic Alliance (NDA), including the Asom Gana Parishad (AGP) and Bodoland People's Front (BPF), seeks a third consecutive term in government.

articles.title: RBI releases norms for banks holding non financial assets
articles.summary: The Reserve Bank of India has released new draft rules. Banks can now hold specific non-financial assets only to recover bad loans. These assets must be sold within seven years. Selling them back to borrowers is prohibited. This aims for transparency and better recovery. The rules are open for public comment until May 26.
articles.raw_summary: The Reserve Bank of India has released new draft rules. Banks can now hold specific non-financial assets only to recover bad loans. These assets must be sold within seven years. Selling them back to borrowers is prohibited. This aims for transparency and better recovery. The rules are open for public comment until May 26.

articles.title: Comms expert Prasoon Joshi appointed chairman of Prasar Bharati
articles.summary: The Government of India has appointed communications expert Prasoon Joshi as the chairman of Prasar Bharati, the country's public broadcaster. Prasar Bharati operates Doordarshan and All India Radio, playing a key role in India's media landscape. The appointment is made under the Prasar Bharati Act, 1990, which governs the autonomous body's functioning.

articles.title: Mere Registration Of Cases Without Proof Of Organised Crime Not Sufficient To Establish Guilt Under UP Gangsters Act: Uttarakhand HC
articles.summary: The Uttarakhand High Court has held that mere registration of previous cases or criminal antecedents, without proof of an organised pattern of criminal activity, is not sufficient to establish guilt under the Uttar Pradesh Gangsters and Anti-Social Activities (Prevention) Act, 1986. The Court observed that the prosecution must establish the existence of a gang, continuity of unlawful activity, and the involvement of the accused in such activity in furtherance of a common object. Justice Ashish N

articles.title: ADB warns Asian economies against broad fuel subsidies, tax cuts amid oil price risks
articles.summary: The Asian Development Bank (ADB) advised Asian economies, including India, to avoid broad fuel subsidies and tax cuts due to rising crude oil prices, warning they could strain public finances. Instead, it recommended targeted cash transfers for vulnerable groups and a shift toward green energy. The lender also urged central banks to monitor inflation before tightening monetary policy.
articles.raw_summary: The Asian Development Bank advised Asian economies to avoid broad fuel subsidies and excise duty cuts, warning they could strain public finances amid elevated crude oil prices. The lender suggested targeted cash transfers for vulnerable populations and a shift to green energy, while urging central banks to monitor inflation expectations before tightening monetary policy.

The decision reflects growing concerns over the safety of agricultural chemicals in the country.

articles.title: Why your health insurance won't cover the true cost of ageing
articles.summary: The article highlights that while platforms like Bima Sugam simplify insurance purchases, India's health insurance policies are structured to cover medical emergencies rather than the ongoing expenses tied to ageing. This mismatch leaves many elderly individuals underinsured for long-term care needs. The issue underscores a broader gap in policy design that may require regulatory or legislative attention.
articles.raw_summary: While platforms such as Bima Sugam make buying insurance easier, better access doesn't solve the deeper problem: policies are designed for medical crises, not the recurring, ongoing costs of ageing.

The decision highlights the conflict between a survivor's choice and the unborn baby's right to life. The case underscores the need for clearer legal frameworks on abortion rights in India.

articles.title: Freebie burden may strain state finances, push borrowing higher: Economists
articles.summary: Economists warn that rising welfare spending by Indian states, including subsidies and freebies, could strain state finances and force cuts to capital expenditure. M R Madhavan, Co-founder of PRS Legislative Research, highlights that a large portion of state revenues is already committed to salaries, pensions, interest payments, and subsidies, leaving little room for discretionary spending. He notes this could ultimately push states to borrow more.
articles.raw_summary: M R Madhavan, Co-founder & Pres, PRS Legislative Research, and who has previously worked as an economist with I-Sec, ICICI and Bank of America, said states may ultimately be forced to squeeze capital expenditure if welfare spending rises while revenues disappoint. He noted that a large portion of state revenues is already committed towards salaries, pensions, interest payments and subsidies, leaving limited room for discretionary spending.

articles.title: Recycled Activewear Reveals the Structural Limits of Fashion’s Sustainability Claims
articles.summary: The article examines the sustainability claims of recycled polyester in activewear, noting that most material still comes from PET drink bottles rather than discarded textiles. This raises concerns about whether fashion brands are truly addressing waste or merely shifting it to other recycling systems. The issue has implications for global textile waste management and could influence future environmental and trade policies in India.
articles.raw_summary: Recycled polyester has become a default sustainability claim across activewear, swimwear and outerwear, but its supply chain is less circular than its labelling suggests. Most of the material still comes from PET drink bottles, not discarded textiles, raising difficult questions about whether fashion is solving waste or diverting it from better-functioning recycling systems globally instead.

As per the ECI's final counting numbers, Adhikari of the Bharatiya Janata Party has secured 73,917 votes (53.02%), ahead of Banerjee of the All India Trinamool Congress, who has polled 58,812 votes (42.19%), a lead of 15,105 votes.

As per the ECI's final counting numbers, Adhikari of the Bharatiya Janata Party has secured 73,917 votes (53.02%), ahead of Banerjee of the All India Trinamool Congress, who has polled 58,812 votes (42.19%), a lead of 15,105 votes.

articles.title: Welfare spending surge pushes subsidy outgo to 91% of target
articles.summary: The Indian government's welfare spending has surged, with food subsidies reaching ₹1.92 trillion (85% of the revised target) and urea subsidies exceeding the annual target at ₹1.28 trillion (101% of revised estimates). Urea subsidy outgo is also higher than the previous year's ₹1.16 trillion. This reflects increased expenditure on social welfare programs, particularly food and agricultural subsidies, which may prompt policy reviews or adjustments.
articles.raw_summary: Food subsidy, the largest component, stood at ₹1.92 trillion, or 85% of the revised estimate of ₹2.28 trillion. However, the urea subsidy has already breached its annual target, reaching ₹1.28 trillion, or 101% of the revised estimates, and higher than the ₹1.16 trillion recorded a year ago.

articles.title: Congress extends support to Vijay’s TVK, with a condition—‘no communal forces’ in Tamil Nadu alliance
articles.summary: The Congress party has extended support to the Tamil Maanila Congress (TVK) in Tamil Nadu, but with a condition that the alliance must exclude 'communal forces.' The DMK has criticized Congress for this move, calling it a 'backstabber,' while Congress leaders argue they are merely supporting the people's choice. The development highlights political tensions and potential implications for future electoral alliances in the state.

articles.title: Sugarcane FRP hiked to Rs 365/quintal for 2026-27 season
articles.summary: The Indian government has increased the Fair and Remunerative Price (FRP) for sugarcane to ₹365 per quintal for the 2026-27 season. Farmers will receive an additional ₹3.56 per quintal for every 0.1% increase in sugar recovery above this level, with reductions for lower recovery. This decision impacts sugarcane farmers and the sugar industry, aligning pricing with productivity incentives.
articles.raw_summary: Farmers will receive a premium of ₹3.56 per quintal for every 0.1% increase in recovery above this level, with a corresponding reduction for lower recovery.

articles.title: Services, retail lending drives banks’ credit growth to nearly 16% in FY26
articles.summary: India's banking sector saw credit growth of nearly 16% in FY26, driven by strong retail and services lending. Personal loans grew 16.2%, supported by vehicle financing and gold-backed loans, while housing credit remained stable. Personal loans accounted for about a third of total credit. The Reserve Bank of India (RBI) may monitor these trends for potential policy adjustments.
articles.raw_summary: Retail lending remained strong, with personal loans rising 16.2%, up from 11.7% a year earlier, supported by demand for vehicle financing and gold-backed loans, while housing credit growth remained steady. Personal loans accounted for about a third of total credit.

articles.title: “Manifest Arbitrariness”: Allahabad HC grants compensation & damages to man whose vehicle was wrongly seized and auctioned under U.P. Cow Slaughter Act
articles.summary: The Allahabad High Court ruled in favor of a man whose vehicle was wrongfully seized and auctioned under the Uttar Pradesh Cow Slaughter Act. The Court noted that the state proceeded with the auction despite ongoing legal proceedings, causing severe livelihood loss. It granted compensation and damages, highlighting manifest arbitrariness in the state's actions. The ruling underscores the need for adherence to due process in enforcement of laws.
articles.raw_summary: The Court noted that it was equally disturbing that during pendency of proceedings before the Commissioner, the State proceeded to auction the vehicle, which was the principal source of livelihood of the accused, for a grossly inadequate consideration without awaiting adjudication of the accused s challenge. articles.title: AI use surges globally but rich-poor divide widens, Microsoft says
articles.summary: Generative AI usage has grown to 17.8% of the global working-age population, but the gap between wealthy and developing nations is widening. In early 2026, 27.5% of people in developed countries used AI tools compared to 15.4% in developing nations, with the divide increasing by 1.5 percentage points since late 2025. Microsoft's report highlights this disparity as AI adoption accelerates worldwide.
articles.raw_summary: Generative artificial intelligence is being used by 17.8% of the world's working-age population, but the gap between wealthy and developing nations continues to widen, according to a report published Tuesday by Microsoft. In the first quarter of 2026, 27.5% of people aged 15-64 in developed countries used a generative AI tool, compared with 15.4% in the developing world - a gap that widened by 1.5 percentage points from the second half of 2025, according to the report's estimates.

The agreement promises technological and economic advancements for both countries.
